checks.finding: REQ-001 through REQ-011 define deterministic routing inputs, tie-breaks, escalation triggers, isolation behavior, manifest fields, failure diagnostics, and release scope with observable acceptance coverage.

No contradiction was found: REQ-006 permits same-session fallback only for implementation on hosts without subagents, while REQ-005 explicitly forbids reviewer and evaluator fallback, and the acceptance tests preserve that distinction.

AC-001 through AC-007 and TEST-001 through TEST-006 cover equal attempts and costs, differing failure classes, terminal-tier recurrence, unavailable tiers and runtimes, identifier reuse, manifest tampering, symlinks, traversal, mutable inputs, malformed cost provenance, and rollback failure.

Host capability, task risk, cost provenance, runtime availability, subagent support, and human reapproval assumptions are converted into checked registries, policies, manifest evidence, deterministic prechecks, or explicit fail-closed outcomes.

REQ-004 reserves terminal-tier resume to the human authority permitted to approve tasks and requires a diagnosis reference, contract revision, and explicit reapproval; the project workflow separately retains human task approval before implementation.

The specification supplies observable contracts for routing, escalation, context isolation, validator equivalence, reporting, release validation, and rollback, enabling design and task decomposition without requiring implementation details at this gate.
